conditions.text: The single phase equivalent circuit of a three-phase, four pole induction motor is shown in Figure 1.
The machine is connected to a three-phase voltage source with line-neutral voltage of 200V RMS (346V line-line) and a frequency of 400 radians/second.
The motor reaches peak torque at a speed of 160 radians/second (about 1528 RPM).

targets.text: What is the value of that peak torque (in N-m)?

answer_slots.target_text: What is the value of that peak torque (in N-m)?

sentence_segments.text: The single phase equivalent circuit of a three-phase, four pole induction motor is shown in Figure 1.
For the purposes of this problem we will assume armature resistance is negligible.
The machine is connected to a three-phase voltage source with line-neutral voltage of 200V RMS (346V line-line) and a frequency of 400 radians/second.
The motor reaches peak torque at a speed of 160 radians/second (about 1528 RPM).
What is the value of that peak torque (in N-m)?

conditions.text: The figure illustrates a cascaded system of two water tanks.
- into the first tank at a rate \( r_0(t) \),
- out of the first tank and into the second at a rate \( r_1(t) \), and
- out of the second tank at a rate \( r_2(t) \).

The rate of flow out of each tank is proportional to the height of the water in that tank: \( r_1(t) = k_1 h_1(t) \) and \( r_2(t) = k_2 h_2(t) \), where \( k_1 \) and \( k_2 \) are each \( 0.2 \, \text{m}^2/\text{second} \).
Both tanks have heights of 1m.
The cross-sectional area of tank 1 is \( A_1 = 4 \, \text{m}^2 \) and that of the second tank is \( A_2 = 2 \, \text{m}^2 \).
At time \( t = 0 \), both tanks are empty.
Assume that \( r_0(t) \) is held constant at rate \( r_0 \).

targets.text: What is the maximum value of \( r_0 \) such that neither tank will ever overflow if both tanks start out empty?

answer_slots.target_text: What is the maximum value of \( r_0 \) such that neither tank will ever overflow if both tanks start out empty?

sentence_segments.text: The figure illustrates a cascaded system of two water tanks.
Water flows:
- into the first tank at a rate \( r_0(t) \),
- out of the first tank and into the second at a rate \( r_1(t) \), and
- out of the second tank at a rate \( r_2(t) \).

The rate of flow out of each tank is proportional to the height of the water in that tank: \( r_1(t) = k_1 h_1(t) \) and \( r_2(t) = k_2 h_2(t) \), where \( k_1 \) and \( k_2 \) are each \( 0.2 \, \text{m}^2/\text{second} \).
Both tanks have heights of 1m.
The cross-sectional area of tank 1 is \( A_1 = 4 \, \text{m}^2 \) and that of the second tank is \( A_2 = 2 \, \text{m}^2 \).
At time \( t = 0 \), both tanks are empty.
Assume that \( r_0(t) \) is held constant at rate \( r_0 \).
What is the maximum value of \( r_0 \) such that neither tank will ever overflow if both tanks start out empty?
